title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bobby continues his deception with Mrs. Rivington, using the pretext of handling the legal affairs of Mr. Castles and discussing fluctuating dollar exchange rates to gather information. Mrs. Rivington reveals that Castles, a Canadian, appeared despondent after visiting the Baskerville-French residence. She also notes that Castles had asked unusual and intrusive questions regarding a neighbor, Dr. Nicholson, and his wife. After leaving, Bobby reflects that his impersonation has likely damaged the reputation of Dolly Martlew.

Bobby and Frankie later discuss the implications of Castles' behavior. While Frankie suggests his visit to the Baskerville-Frenches might have been coincidental, Bobby believes Castles may have intentionally sought them out after overhearing information regarding their lunch plans.

Frankie returns to Stavely, where she enters the study and finds Dr. Nicholson holding Sylvia Baxton-French's hands. A distraught Sylvia rushes past Frankie in tears, stating that the doctor has just delivered terrible news. Frankie observes that Dr. Nicholson is masking his visible anger behind a polite facade. The doctor expresses a desire for Mrs. Baskin-French to persuade her husband to allow him to handle the situation.

Meanwhile, Bobby returns to his hotel and is directed to a sitting room to meet a visitor. He discovers the girl from the photograph, dressed in black and trembling with fear. The girl questions whether she should have come and doubts Bobby's ability to help him. Bobby reassures her that she is safe and promises his assistance. Noticing his composed demeanor, the girl realizes that Bobby is not merely a driver.
